fix: restrict group collapse/expand to chevron click only

Prevent accidental collapse when clicking elsewhere on the group
header (e.g. after editing a cell). Only the chevron arrow button
triggers toggle now.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

design: minimal type-aware empty states for all editable cells

Replace verbose "Cliquer pour modifier..." with quiet type-specific
indicators: dash for text/number, persistent icons for date/phone/
email/location, muted User icon without text, "—" for empty selects.
Icons always visible as anchors so empty→filled transition is smooth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: replace DossierFilters with ColumnFilters on dossiers page

Extract matchesFilter to shared utility, update dossiers page to use
the same per-column filtering system as board pages. Remove old
DossierFilters component (status + date range only) in favor of
ColumnFilters (type-aware operators for all column types).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

design: add dark/light theme system with next-themes

Install next-themes, add ThemeProvider + ThemeToggle, define CSS
design tokens for both modes in globals.css, migrate ~1500 hardcoded
colors to semantic tokens across all components. Sidebar is light in
light mode, dark navy in dark mode. All color-coded elements (badges,
file types, status indicators) use dark: variants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- request/route.ts: skickar uppgifter via Telegram Bot API till clawdbot-sh
  (WebSocket fungerar ej från Vercel serverless)
- request/route.ts: sparar tasks i Supabase istf in-memory Map
- status/route.ts: läser från Supabase istf mock/in-memory
- callback/route.ts: uppdaterar clawdbot_tasks → triggar Realtime
- ChatWidget.tsx: Supabase Realtime subscription — öppnar och notifierar

- 016_clawdbot_tasks.sql: ny tabell för persistent task-tracking + Realtime

commit.message:
Add grants-scanner: AI-powered funding opportunity monitor (#4)

* Add grants-scanner: AI-powered public funding opportunity monitor

Standalone Next.js app that automatically scans 20+ Spanish, EU, and multilateral
funding sources for grants, subsidies, and procurement opportunities relevant to
T4G Lab. Uses Claude to extract opportunities from web pages and score them into
three priority tiers (High Priority / Interesting / Unlikely).

Features:
- Dashboard with sortable, resizable columns and inline editing
- Three-tier priority system with visual badges
- Smart extraction filtering (excludes irrelevant scholarships/welfare)
- Soft delete (dismissed opportunities never reappear)
- Manual URL addition for ad-hoc opportunity scanning
- Background scan execution with progress polling
- Email alerts via Resend for new opportunities
- Configurable scan schedule and company context

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add applications feature with AI-powered analysis and progress tracking

- "Apply" button on dashboard sends opportunity to Claude for deep analysis
- Claude returns: requirements, documentation needed, funding ideas, complexity, application URL
- Applications page with status tracker (Research → Prepare → Draft → Submit → Review → Result)
- Editable notes, status updates, sortable table
- Fix analyzer bug: preserve original fields (amount, deadline) instead of trusting Claude to return them
- Resizable table columns with drag handles
- Priority badge nowrap fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Raul Saenz <raul@Rauls-Mac-mini.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Next.js only recognizes src/middleware.ts as middleware; the file was
incorrectly named proxy.ts and the /app/* routes were unprotected.
Also rename exported function from proxy → middleware.

Add --with-dotfiles flag, default dotfiles off

Dotfile mounting (.zshrc, .gitconfig, .tmux.conf, etc.) is now opt-in
via --with-dotfiles, config key with_dotfiles, or .ss-dotfiles marker.

skipped unless enabled.

commit.message:
F4.2: Implement Use Case Semantic Matching & Top-10 Display

Created comprehensive semantic matching system:

SemanticMatcher class with:
- score_similarity(): Keyword overlap + keyword/title/description scoring
- get_top_matches(): Rank all 40+ use cases by relevance
- format_top_10_display(): Hinglish top-10 with progress bars
- format_full_catalog(): Complete categorized catalog display
- handle_use_case_selection(): ID-based selection parsing
- quick_suggest(): Auto-suggest if confidence > 0.75

Integration into mode_gate.py:
- conversation_entry() now uses semantic matching for GUIDED mode
- Shows top-10 contextual use cases
- Falls back to full catalog when no context
- Returns matched use case IDs for next step

Features:
- Scoring: Keywords (40%) + Title (30%) + Description (30%)
- Top-10 ranking with confidence percentage
- Progress bar visualization (█░)
- Hinglish prompts for user guidance
- Domain-based full catalog view
- Expert assignment per use case

Tested and verified:
- Salary negotiation query correctly ranks C5/F3 at top
- Interview queries show C1-C3 matches
- Full catalog displays all 7 domains
- Integration with conversation entry points works

Unblocks: F4.3, F4.4, F4.5, F4.6, F7.2, F7.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add ESP32 device pairing, theme import, and Christmas theme

Device pairing (no camera required):
- Device model, schema, and router (/api/devices, /api/device/{code}/poll)
- ESP32 derives a 4-char code from its MAC address and displays it on screen
- Parent enters the code in the new Device tab to link it to a child's portal
- Device polls the server every 30 s and renders messages via LVGL
- WiFiManager captive portal for first-time WiFi + server URL setup

ESP32 firmware (LILYGO T-Display-S3 AMOLED 1.91"):
- Full Arduino sketch with state machine (pairing → displaying)
- Parses theme colours from API response to colour the LVGL screen
- NTP clock, scrolling text, long-press BOOT button to reset
- PlatformIO config included

Theme import (admin):
- POST /api/themes/import — bulk upsert by key
- Admin UI: paste JSON, live preview with new/update badges, one-click import

Christmas / Santa theme:
- themes/christmas_santa.json ready to paste into the import UI
- PublicDisplay: christmas key gets snowflake particles and Santa scene
  (🎅 rocket across sky, 🦌 floating, ⛄ bouncing, 🎄 wiggling, ⭐ glowing)

commit.message:
feat: add /app page — browser-based encrypted text messaging

Full HYVE encryption stack running client-side in the browser:
- X3DH key exchange + Double Ratchet (byte-compatible with Android app)
- Shamir K-of-N key sharding over 512-byte cells (GF(256), AES poly)
- ChaCha20-Poly1305 cell encryption with blind routing tokens
- PBKDF2+AES-GCM vault (PIN-protected identity in IndexedDB)
- WebSocket relay + hyve-id HTTP client
- 4-view state machine: setup → locked → home → chat
- iOS users can now send free encrypted texts without installing anything
- Cross-platform: web ↔ Android users share same relay + hyve-id servers

commit.message:
Feat: OAuth Flow de Slack completo (FASE 3 - Parte 2)

## 🎯 OAuth Flow Completamente Funcional

### Endpoints API Implementados
- ✅ GET /api/slack/oauth/start - Iniciar OAuth con Slack
- ✅ GET /api/slack/oauth/callback - Procesar autorización
- ✅ POST /api/slack/disconnect - Desconectar workspace
- ✅ GET /api/slack/status - Estado de conexión actual

### UI de Configuración de Slack
- ✅ Página /dashboard/slack completa
- ✅ Estado de conexión en tiempo real
- ✅ Botón "Conectar con Slack" funcional
- ✅ Información del workspace conectado
- ✅ Botón de desconexión
- ✅ Alertas de éxito/error
- ✅ Loading states
- ✅ Diseño responsive

### Características OAuth
- ✅ OAuth 2.0 flow completo
- ✅ State token para prevenir CSRF
- ✅ 15 bot scopes configurados
- ✅ 2 user scopes configurados
- ✅ Validación de credenciales
- ✅ Manejo robusto de errores
- ✅ Almacenamiento en BD (slack_connections)
- ✅ Verificación de permisos (owner/admin)

### Scopes Configurados
Bot Scopes:
- chat:write, chat:write.public
- channels:history, channels:read
- groups:history, groups:read
- im:history, im:read, im:write
- mpim:history, mpim:read, mpim:write
- app_mentions:read, users:read, team:read

User Scopes:
- identity.basic, identity.email

### Documentación
- ✅ GUIA-SLACK-APP-SETUP.md - Guía paso a paso completa
- ✅ RESUMEN-OAUTH-IMPLEMENTADO.md - Documentación técnica
- ✅ .env.example - Template de variables de entorno
- ✅ PROGRESO-FASE-3.md actualizado

### Mejoras al Dashboard Principal
- ✅ Botón "Conectar Slack" ahora redirige a /dashboard/slack
- ✅ Acción rápida funcional

bench: yoga baselines — decode 0.97x parity, CUDA 12.6 + provable contracts (Refs paiml/infra#1)

Yoga (RTX 4060 Laptop, sm_89) baselines established for all 3 runtimes:
- realizr: 138.7 decode tok/s, 114.4ms TTFT, 7.2ms ITL
- llama.cpp: 143.4 decode tok/s, 12.1ms TTFT, 7.0ms ITL (sm_89, CUDA 12.6)

Replace MemoryStream with GrowableBuffer in ORC write pipeline for span-based I/O

Introduces a non-virtual GrowableBuffer that replaces MemoryStream across all ORC
encoders and column writers, enabling span-based varint writes and eliminating
ToArray() copies. Benchmarks show 5-18% faster writes and 8-15% less allocation.

commit.message:
v10.1.2178: Global creation+facts pipeline — selective resolver skip, generic chain expansion, non-blocking follow-ups

TS-FIX-GLOBAL-CREATION-PLUS-FACTS-PIPELINE-V1:

1. Selective resolver skip: Creation actions now skip resolution ONLY for the
   entity being created (via CREATION_SKIP_ENTITY_TYPES mapping). Other entity
   references (crop, variety, material, asset) still resolve normally.

2. Generic post-creation fact expansion: _expand_creation_followups() builds
   chains from CREATION_FOLLOWUP_MAP. For add_field: ADD_FIELD → SET_FIELD_CROP
   → SET_FIELD_VARIETY when crop/variety facts are present.

3. Non-blocking chain execution: After creation (step 0) succeeds, follow-up
   steps are best-effort — failures are recorded but don't block remaining steps.

4. Generic entity ID propagation: Created entity IDs (field_id, asset_id,
   material_id) are injected into facts for downstream chain steps.

5. Truthful result aggregation: partial_success flag when primary succeeds but
   follow-ups fail. Mouth receives both success and failure intent_results.

6. Singular→array promotion and multi-entity clarification checks now only
   skip for the entity type being created, not all entity types.

commit.message:
Fix sticky header offsets not updating when header collapses

Sub-header (Front 9/Back 9) and table thead had fixed top offsets
based on expanded header height. When header collapsed on scroll,
offsets weren't recalculated, causing headers to drift down.
Now recalculates after collapse/expand.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: rewrite cms-wp as multi-approach prompt with Gutenberg blocks

Complete rewrite of the CMS editability prompt. Now asks the user
which editing experience they want before proceeding:

A) Gutenberg Blocks (recommended) — custom blocks per section,
   inline RichText editing, MediaUpload, InnerBlocks for repeaters,
   block patterns for page templates, @wordpress/scripts toolchain
B) ACF Field Groups — form-based admin editing (original approach)
C) ACF Blocks — hybrid (ACF fields inside Gutenberg editor)
D) Headless — WordPress as API-only backend

Approach saved to .treble/cms-plan.json so it doesn't ask again.

Gutenberg section covers: block.json metadata, edit.js with
RichText/MediaUpload/InspectorControls, render.php with $attributes,
InnerBlocks pattern for repeatable items, CPT with template lock,
page patterns for pre-assembled layouts, build toolchain setup.

Visual verification (two-tier) aligned with dev-shadcn format across
all approaches.

commit.message:
Switch Colab to Qwen3-4B-Base (text-only) and disable torch dynamo

Qwen3.5-4B is a VLM — its visual encoder causes matmul dimension
mismatches in Unsloth's compiled GRPO trainer. Qwen3-4B-Base is
text-only with the same parameter count and works correctly.

Also fully disables torch.dynamo to prevent compiled cache errors.

fix(sw): bump caché v5→v6 para purgar chunks obsoletos en dispositivos móviles

El Service Worker usa cache-first para /_next/static/*. Usuarios que visitaron
la demo antes del fix del xsLayout tienen el bundle viejo cacheado. Al cambiar
la versión del caché, el SW elimina todos los caches anteriores en `activate`
y los clientes obtienen el código nuevo.

commit.message:
v0.5.0: card lifecycle, polished English copy, bug fixes

Card lifecycle system:
- Generate form → waiting card (blue) → candidate card (green) or error card (red)
- Regenerate / Route Regen send waiting card, update in-place on completion
- Use update_card instead of delete_message (avoids "recalled" notification)
- Extract _build_waiting_card() helper to eliminate duplication

UX improvements:
- Rewrite all user-facing messages to polished English copy
- Unify separator from | to · (middle dot) across all cards/messages
- Enforce field order: region > price > subject > others (e.g. deadline)
- Schedule loading indicator: "Let me look into it for a sec. 🔎"
- "STOP" in edit flow now clears session instead of triggering edit
- Download done emoji: ↓ → 👇

Card design system:
- Colors: green=success, red=failure, blue=interactive, purple=info
- Structure: title=status, subtitle=guidance, body=interaction
- Request success card: new title/subtitle, lark_md hyperlink to Gift Schedule
- Candidate card desc reordered: region · price · subject
- Remove stale Chinese fallback in Gemini edit provider

Data/infra:
- Delivered view: separate bitable view (vewxjHwUBm) for delivered count
- Schedule metrics: "X waiting for us", "X% delivered"
- Gemini edit: retry on empty response + finishReason diagnostics
